title: Human as the No-Arbiter Species
one_sentence: This module defines human as no-arbiter recognition plus editability under institutional reversal.

core_compression.human: system that recognizes not exists Arbiter_external
core_compression.institution: provisional cost device, not ultimate arbiter
core_compression.editability: Apply(Edit) to C when Reversal(C) is detected
core_compression.responsibility: redesign(C) so closure paths become more costly than openness paths

minimal_signals_of_reversal: enforcement optimizes obedience rather than openness

failure_modes.F18_1: no-arbiter recognition decays -> institution absolutization -> coercive closure
failure_modes.F18_2: editability becomes infeasible -> modification cost explodes -> closure attractor locks in
failure_modes.F18_3: editing is corrupted into justification -> closure rationalized rather than repaired

loop_closure.claim: No external arbiter implies institutions must remain provisional; editability restores openness when stabilizers reverse into closure.

negative_constraints: Do not treat this as biological human essence.
Do not treat this as human superiority.
Do not use as legal constitutional theory.
Do not use as case study of specific states, religions, or historical events without evidence routing.
